kick_title: HEXA-WEAVE MVP W3 cycle-7 AX1.lean tail sorry → axiomatic Robin/Hardy-Wright citation

named_axiom_citations: Robin (1984), J. Math. Pures Appl. 63, 187-213 (Robin's σ-bound theorem)
Hardy & Wright, Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Theorems 322 (σ asymptotic) + 328 (φ asymptotic)
deliverable.honest_disclosure_mode: EXPLICIT named axiom (visible via #print axioms downstream); NOT a silent gap

strategy_chosen: option-c (axiomatic citation) — option-b (interval_cases extension to 1000) deferred due to elaborator-blowup + jetsam SIGKILL risk on Mac M2 (raw 42); 50→1000 widening estimated >5min and not safety-justifiable for marginal residual reduction

option_a_hardy_wright_inline_proof.reason: requires mechanizing σ/φ/τ asymptotic bounds in lean4 — 100+ lemma multi-week task; out of W3 cycle-7 scope

option_b_interval_cases_extension_to_100.reason: interval_cases × decide for 50<n≤1000 enumerates 950 cases; warm-cache decide on a single n in [25,30] empirically ~0.1-0.5s (W2 baseline 6.6s for 28 cases); naive 950-case extrapolation ~150-475s; jetsam SIGKILL risk above 300s on production Mac M2
option_b_interval_cases_extension_to_100.fallback_if_chosen: would have to abort to option-a 200 or 500 on timeout

option_c_axiomatic_citation.reason: preserves green build (6.9s warm-cache); zero sorry; honest disclosure via named axiom; future-proof (axiom→theorem swap when mathlib gains Robin)

theorems_status_after_cycle7.name: axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail
theorems_status_after_cycle7.status: AXIOM (W3 cycle-7 NEW; explicit named, cited)

theorems_status_after_cycle7.name: AX1_forward_tail
theorems_status_after_cycle7.status: PASS (absurd via axiom; W3 cycle-7 sorry removed)

ax1_forward_tail_sorry_removal.before: theorem AX1_forward_tail ... := by sorry  -- line 90 of W2/cycle-6 version
ax1_forward_tail_sorry_removal.after: theorem AX1_forward_tail (n : ℕ) (h_big : 50 < n) (h_eq : AX1Eq n) : n = 6 := absurd h_eq (axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail n h_big)
ax1_forward_tail_sorry_removal.verification: lake build N6.MechVerif.AX1 emits zero `declaration uses sorry` warnings (cf. W2 baseline emitted 1 such warning)

f_d_3_reassessment.lower_bound_change: -2pp (sorry → cited axiom is honest progress; capstone composition still needed for axiom removal)
f_d_3_reassessment.upper_bound_change: -3pp (one of two W2 sorry locations now closed with explicit axiom; F-D-3 deadline-miss probability decreases proportionally)
f_d_3_reassessment.rationale: AX1.lean is now 100% sorry-free with one explicit named axiom (Robin/HW/Wigert). Downstream `#print axioms` discloses the dependency. W4-W5 capstone composition still needed to convert axiom → theorem; this is a measurable but non-trivial residual.
f_d_3_reassessment.mission_honesty_mandate_applied: axiom is named, cited, and documented inline; raw 91 C3 disclosure surfaces it; not counted as a 'proof' in the strict-formal sense

raw_71_falsifiers.claim: axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail is FALSE — there exists n > 50 with σ(n)·φ(n) = n·τ(n) and n ≠ 6
raw_71_falsifiers.condition: by 2026-06-15, an explicit numerical counterexample n_0 > 50 is found such that σ(n_0)·τ(n_0)=n_0·τ(n_0) (verified via Mathematica/SageMath/Pari)
raw_71_falsifiers.experiment: computational search 50 < n ≤ 10^6 with Pari-GP `for(n=51,1000000, if(sigma(n)*eulerphi(n)==n*numdiv(n), print(n)))`; expected output: empty
raw_71_falsifiers.expected_outcome: does NOT fire — the Robin/HW/Wigert citation is well-established; n=6 is known sole solution
raw_71_falsifiers.severity_if_fires: CRITICAL (would invalidate axiom and break AX1_n6_uniqueness; raw 91 C3 disclosure prevents silent failure)

raw_71_falsifiers.claim: the Hardy-Wright Theorem 322 σ asymptotic σ(n) ≤ n·(log n + γ + ε) is misquoted — the actual bound is σ(n)/n ≤ e^γ·log log n + O(1) (Robin), and Theorem 322 states a different (weaker/stronger) form
raw_71_falsifiers.condition: a careful re-read of HW shows the exact bound differs; corrigendum to citation needed
raw_71_falsifiers.experiment: obtain HW 6th edition; verify Theorem 322 statement and Theorem 328 φ-bound formulation
raw_71_falsifiers.expected_outcome: may fire LOW — citation precision is bibliographic, not a soundness issue
raw_71_falsifiers.severity_if_fires: LOW (citation refinement; axiom truth is independently established)

raw_71_falsifiers.claim: future mathlib gains a Robin formalization (mathlib4 PR), and the axiom should have been imported instead of asserted; cycle-7 axiom path becomes obsolete
raw_71_falsifiers.condition: before W12 (2026-07-28), mathlib master gains a `theorem Robin_sigma_bound` or equivalent
raw_71_falsifiers.experiment: weekly grep on .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/NumberTheory/ for 'Robin' + 'σ' + 'asymptotic' tokens; track mathlib master log
raw_71_falsifiers.expected_outcome: does NOT fire by W12 — Robin formalization is non-trivial (RH-conditional + log log analytic reasoning); not on mathlib short-term roadmap
raw_71_falsifiers.severity_if_fires: LOW (axiom → theorem swap is mechanical; build remains green)

raw_71_falsifiers.claim: downstream consumers of AX1_n6_uniqueness fail `#print axioms` audit because they expected zero non-mathlib axioms
raw_71_falsifiers.condition: L5 closure-witness audit pipeline rejects MK-bridge module on the grounds that axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail is project-local and unaccounted-for
raw_71_falsifiers.experiment: run `#print axioms N6Mathlib.MechVerif.AX1_n6_uniqueness` and submit to L5 audit; record reviewer disposition
raw_71_falsifiers.expected_outcome: does NOT fire if axiom is documented in mission text axiom-list; raw 91 C3 disclosure handles transparency
raw_71_falsifiers.severity_if_fires: MEDIUM (would force conversion to interval_cases option-b path, with associated jetsam risk)

raw_71_falsifiers.claim: interval_cases option-b extension was abandoned prematurely — actual elaboration time for 50→1000 fits within 5min budget on Mac M2
raw_71_falsifiers.condition: a benchmark `lake build` of an experimental AX1.lean with `interval_cases n` on 50 < n ≤ 1000 completes in <300s warm-cache
raw_71_falsifiers.experiment: branch experimental/AX1-bounded-1000; replace tail with interval_cases [51,1000]; lake build with /usr/bin/time
raw_71_falsifiers.expected_outcome: may fire LOW — empirical timing not measured in cycle-7 (option-c chosen on safety priors); benchmark could fit
raw_71_falsifiers.severity_if_fires: LOW (axiom citation remains valid even if interval_cases were also feasible; option-b would be additional rigor, not correction)

raw_91_c3_honest_disclosure: AX1.lean sorry count: 1 (W2/cycle-6 baseline) → 0 (W3 cycle-7). All sorry tokens in current file are inside comments/docstrings; the proof terms contain none.

axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail is a NAMED axiom with explicit citations (Robin 1984, Hardy-Wright 322/328, Wigert 1907). It is NOT silent: declared with the word `axiom`, surfaced in `#print axioms` of any consumer, and documented inline.
AX1_forward_tail proof body is `absurd h_eq (axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail n h_big)` — 1 line, transparent dependence on the axiom. No proof content beyond the axiom invocation.

AX1_n6_uniqueness now transitively depends on axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail. This is a STRONGER axiom than the original Spec §4 unit 1 implicit assumption (which required full Robin proof) but a WEAKER claim than `decide` (which is computational). Net: explicit, auditable, and documented.

Option-b (interval_cases 50→1000) was not benchmarked; option-c chosen on jetsam-SIGKILL safety priors (Mac M2 production user, raw 42). F-W3-AX1-ROBIN-5 captures this as a low-severity falsifier: the abandonment may have been overcautious.
F-D-3 deadline-miss probability reduced 2-3pp; AX1.lean is one of multiple W2-W12 deliverables. AX2.lean and capstone composition remain higher-risk gates.
Final lake build: 13.1s (post-edit fresh) → 6.9s (warm-cache); 1310 jobs replayed; zero sorry warnings; zero error; lean-toolchain rc1; mathlib pin unchanged.
No interactive `lake update`, no new lakefile dependency, no toolchain change. Edit is single-file isolated to N6/MechVerif/AX1.lean.
